highlights: Free entry beach with coastal walking path connecting to Cat Co 2 and 3 beaches
Soft sandy beach with calm, shallow water suitable for swimming and children
Scenic rock formations and evening sunset views over Lạn Hạ Bay

thingsToKnow.crowds: Low season (October-November) is quieter with fewer visitors. High season becomes very crowded. Afternoon periods busier than mornings.
thingsToKnow.safety: Parking fee disputes reported: guards may demand 10,000-20,000 VND parking fees inconsistently. Park away from resort entrance if concerned.
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Concrete paths and steps connect beaches. Small beach area, especially at high tide when sand nearly disappears.
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Water temperature varies by season. November-early October offers best conditions. Summer months become overcrowded.

visitorTips: Visit during low season (October-November) or early morning to avoid crowds and enjoy peaceful atmosphere
Park motorcycle/bike away from resort entrance to avoid inconsistent parking fee demands from guards
Bring cash for chair rentals (200,000 VND for loungers), umbrellas, toilets (20,000 VND), and showers

faq.question: What are the parking fees and charges at Cat Co 1 Beach?
faq.answer: Motorbike parking: 10,000-20,000 VND (inconsistently enforced). Beach chair/lounger rental: 200,000 VND. Toilet/shower use: 20,000 VND. Free entry to beach itself.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit Cat Co 1 Beach?
faq.answer: October-November offers ideal weather and fewer crowds. Avoid summer for overcrowding. Visit mornings or evenings for quieter experience.

faq.question: What water conditions should I expect?
faq.answer: Water is shallow and calm, good for swimming. High tide significantly reduces sand area. Cat Co 1 has softer sand and cleaner water than Cat Co 2 beach nearby.

sentiment.positive: Most visitors praise the beach's natural scenery, rock formations, coastal walking paths, calm shallow water, and peaceful low-season atmosphere.
sentiment.negative: Complaints include plastic/trash in water and on beach, inconsistent parking fee enforcement, loud hotel music, overcrowding in high season, and tidal changes reducing beach space.
